other_observers: Michael Kiessig and Rob Raker on 11/03/2011.; Yes, I looked for the bird on the evening of 11/02/2011 after a storm, but did not see it. Went back the next morning with Michael and Rob and we saw the bird for the first time this season. A Golden-crowned Sparrow had spent the past two winters at Red Rocks and I was anticipating it might return.
specific_location: The feeders behind the Red Rocks Trading Post in Red Rocks Park. N 39deg39'49.6" W105deg.12'09.7"
description: Large sparrow of genus Zonotrichia with heavy body and long tail. Bi-colored bill with gray upper mandible and lighter colored lower mandible. Thick black supercilium with lemon yellow forehead, and gray hindcrown/nape. Mostly non-descript body with gray cheeks/auriculars and breast. Light brown on flanks, brown rump, and pink legs. Brown back with black zig-zag stripes. Wings are a combination of dark brown, black and olive green with 2 faint white wingbars.Behaviors: The bird was seen numerous times at the feeders behind the Red Rocks Trading Post. The feeders are adjacent to a very brushy area with some water seepage. The Golden-crowned would venture out from the brushy area to feed on the ground for a brief period, then scurry back to the overgrown tangle.Call: did not hear bird sing.Plumage: adult nonbreeding
